Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Embracing Bayesian methods in clinical trials 51 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Can It Resolve DOOM? Game Engine in 2k DNS Records 29 upvotes, 2 comments
  3. Explore the Hidden World of Sand 140 upvotes, 30 comments
  4. Meow.camera 126 upvotes, 28 comments
  5. Browser-based SFX synthesizer using WASM/Zig 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  6. Building FireStriker: Making Civic Tech Free 62 upvotes, 13 comments
  7. Installing a Let's Encrypt TLS certificate on a Brother printer with Certbot 162 upvotes, 45 comments
  8. If you don't opt out by Apr 24 GitHub will train on your private repos 117 upvotes, 34 comments
  9. ISBN Visualization – Annas Archive 17 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Nashville library launches Memory Lab for digitizing home movies 47 upvotes, 12 comments
  11. Desk for people who work at home with a cat 281 upvotes, 114 comments
  12. Velxio 2.0 – Emulate Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi 3 in the Browser 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Gzip decompression in 250 lines of Rust 93 upvotes, 35 comments
  14. Anatomy of the .claude/ folder 306 upvotes, 156 comments
  15. Capability-Based Security for Redox: Namespace and CWD as Capabilities 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  16. A Faster Alternative to Jq 348 upvotes, 223 comments
  17. Ask HN: Founders of estonian e-businesses – is it worth it? 59 upvotes, 30 comments
  18. Make macOS consistently bad (unironically) 157 upvotes, 104 comments
  19. Everything old is new again: memory optimization 156 upvotes, 110 comments
  20. The 'paperwork flood': How I drowned a bureaucrat before dinner 486 upvotes, 402 comments
  21. Hold on to Your Hardware 521 upvotes, 434 comments
  22. EMachines never obsolete PCs: More than a meme 52 upvotes, 32 comments
  23. Schedule tasks on the web 275 upvotes, 222 comments
  24. People inside Microsoft are fighting to drop mandatory Microsoft Account 395 upvotes, 338 comments
  25. AI got the blame for the Iran school bombing. The truth is more worrying 264 upvotes, 226 comments
  26. ‘Energy independence feels practical’: Europeans building mini solar farms 161 upvotes, 145 comments
  27. Show HN: Open-Source Animal Crossing–Style UI for Claude Code Agents 30 upvotes, 25 comments
  28. Telnyx package compromised on PyPI 53 upvotes, 67 comments
  29. 21,864 Yugoslavian .yu domains 57 upvotes, 78 comments
  30. Should QA exist? 73 upvotes, 112 comments
